source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A smooth curved trough with mass $3m$ is at rest on a smooth horizontal ground, with its bottom smoothly connected to the horizontal surface. A small ball with mass $m$ slides down from a height $h$ on the trough, starting from rest. When the ball reaches the right wall, it collides with the vertical wall and bounces back with the same speed. The gravitational acceleration is given as $g$.

1. What is the magnitude of the velocity of the small ball when it first collides with the wall?
2. What is the maximum height that the small ball reaches when it slides up the curved groove again?

An image asset exists in the registry and registry_passed=true, so the visual component is available.
multi_step: The problem has two linked asks: speed at first collision and maximum rebound height.
Solving requires combining multiple physical constraints, including motion on a movable trough and then a collision with the wall before the return motion.
The answer consists of two nontrivial symbolic results rather than a direct lookup or single-step computation.
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_present=true; the target outputs are explicit closed-form expressions.
The two answers are precise symbolic quantities (speed and height), which are amenable to rule-based or algebraic checking.
registry_passed=true, so the sample assets and text/answer records are sufficiently registered for downstream verification.
